policy.mandatory_behavior: Every applicable gate with severity blocking must pass before a production release.
policy.not_applicable: Record why the gate cannot apply; absence of evidence is not not-applicable.
policy.waiver: A waiver names owner, reason, user impact, compensating control, expiry date, and follow-up issue.
policy.evidence: Evidence must be reproducible from a named fixture, route, command, capture, or monitoring view.

gates.title: Outcome and requirements
pass_conditions: Primary user, job, action, outcome, and acceptance criteria are named.
Requirements, preferences, hypotheses, and exceptions are separated.
Every material section and feature maps to the brief.

gates.title: Information architecture and content
applies_when: The change adds or alters visible content, navigation, labels, or errors.
pass_conditions: The dominant task and reading sequence are clear.
Labels remain meaningful out of context.
The same user action retains recognizable vocabulary through confirmation, status, result, error, and recovery.
Errors name recovery and do not blame the user.
Representative, empty, long, and failure content is exercised.

gates.title: Visual direction and system coherence
applies_when: The change adds or materially revises visual language.
pass_conditions: Direction is tied to audience, content, and explicit axes.
Signature signals trace to the subject's real world and structural devices encode real relationships.
Signature signals are repeatable and restrained.
Tokens and component roles replace unexplained local values.
A spacious or minimal composition assigns major gaps a hierarchy, pacing, proof, focus, or boundary role.
Anti-pattern detectors have been reviewed rather than ignored.
Substantial generated builds have desktop and mobile captures compared against the design thesis, followed by a documented high-impact refinement pass.
required_evidence: desktop and mobile captures plus refinement notes for substantial generated builds

gates.title: Component states and recovery
applies_when: Any component is interactive, asynchronous, data-driven, selectable, or permission-aware.
pass_conditions: Applicable state-matrix cells are implemented or explicitly impossible.
Transitions, interruption, focus, retry, and preservation are defined.
Visual, DOM, ARIA, and canonical application states agree.

gates.title: Accessibility
pass_conditions: Primary and recovery tasks are keyboard complete.
Name, role, value, state, reading order, and status behavior are correct.
Contrast, focus, large text, non-color cues, reduced motion, and relevant assistive-technology behavior pass.
Automated findings are triaged and complex widgets receive manual review.
required_evidence: accessibility-tree or assistive-technology evidence for complex patterns

gates.title: Responsive layout and input
applies_when: Any user-facing web layout or interaction.
pass_conditions: Continuous resizing reveals no clipping, overlap, missing content, or page-level reading scroll.
Breakpoints correspond to content failure points.
Source, focus, and task order remain coherent.
Keyboard, touch, pointer, zoom, orientation, and virtual keyboard risks are addressed.

gates.title: Performance
applies_when: Any production route or component with measurable loading or interaction cost.
pass_conditions: Route budgets for loading, responsiveness, stability, script, media, and requests pass or have an approved waiver.
Primary content and controls are useful before optional code.
Images and fonts reserve space and deliver appropriate bytes.
Representative constrained-device evidence is included.

gates.title: Browser and platform support
pass_conditions: The declared baseline supports every essential task.
Enhancements use capability detection and usable fallbacks.
Dynamic browser checks establish explicit user-visible readiness, inspect rendered state before action, and assert each result.
High-risk native, input, viewport, accessibility, and rendering behavior is tested on real platforms.

gates.title: Internationalization
applies_when: The product supports or anticipates more than one locale, script, direction, currency, or timezone.
pass_conditions: Pseudo-localized expansion and compact strings do not break tasks.
Locale and timezone formatting is correct and unambiguous.
RTL and mixed-direction content preserve reading, focus, and layout logic.
Fonts and locale resources cover target scripts without unstable loading.

gates.title: Security, privacy, and trust
applies_when: The change handles user data, identity, permissions, destructive actions, claims, or diagnostics.
pass_conditions: Collected, exposed, logged, and retained data is minimized.
Destructive safeguards match consequence and are duplicate-safe.
Errors aid recovery without leakage.
Claims, proof, permissions, and metadata are truthful and current.

gates.title: Anti-slop and reconstruction integrity
applies_when: Any generated, reference-led, screenshot-led, or materially art-directed work.
pass_conditions: Observed, inferred, and assumed decisions are distinguished when fidelity is claimed.
No proof, metrics, logos, content, or states are fabricated.
Cards, pills, gradients, bento, oversized heroes, empty scale, effects, and motion have contextual reasons rather than template defaults.
Alternatives and false-positive rationale were reviewed.
Request-local names and messages are absent from reusable knowledge, public fixtures, public evidence, and packages unless publication was explicitly approved.
required_evidence: configured private-term scan and manual screenshot review when request-local content exists

gates.title: Documentation and maintainability
applies_when: A shared component, token, workflow, or architecture boundary changes.
pass_conditions: Purpose, usage, counterexamples, states, semantics, responsive behavior, performance cost, and browser notes are documented.
Migration and deprecation behavior are documented when public contracts change.
Examples and tests exercise the documented behavior.

gates.title: Release and observability
pass_conditions: Production output—not only development mode—was tested.
Routes, asset paths, metadata, console state, broken links, and horizontal overflow were checked against production output.
Rollback or disable path exists for material risk.
Outcome, error, performance, and critical integrity signals have owners and thresholds.
Known waivers and changes are communicated.
